tree_title: Understanding USAG Gymnastics Levels: A Complete Guide (2024)
overall_topic: Understanding USAG Gymnastics Levels: A Complete Guide (2024)
questions: A gymnastics organization referenced in a 2024 guide to the USA Gymnastics (USAG) level system operates seven training centers across New York and New Jersey to support athletes from developmental through Elite levels. Identify all seven of these locations and provide, for each one, its full street address, phone number, and contact email. Additionally, for the Long Island facility described as being located in a red brick building near a well-known bull statue, provide its regular hours of operation and summarize the main categories of classes and programs offered there.

A multi-location gymnastics training organization in the New York metropolitan area follows the USA Gymnastics (USAG) level system and offers competitive team pathways (including USAG and USAIGC programs) alongside recreational and ninja classes. Focusing only on its New York locations that explicitly feature USAG competitive leadership roles (such as a USAG Head Coach or USAG Team Director), identify the facilities in Centereach, Huntington, and Levittown. For each of these three locations, provide: (1) the full street address, (2) the main contact phone number, (3) the name of the staff member serving in a USAG leadership role at that site, and (4) one notable credential or achievement listed for that individual (e.g., elite competitive background, national titles, university placements, or international competition experience).

An East Coast gymnastics organization that supports athletes progressing through the USA Gymnastics (USAG) Development Program levels (from early developmental stages through optional and elite pathways) operates multiple facilities in New York and New Jersey and offers three distinct competitive team tracks: an in-house club team, a lower-commitment travel program designed for multi-sport athletes, and a higher-commitment Junior Olympic pathway. Identify this organization and provide a complete list of all its current gym locations, including each facility’s city, full street address, phone number, and public contact email. Additionally, describe the three team program options, clearly explaining how their competition structure and weekly training commitments differ.

rounds.answer_a:
The referenced organization is Gold Medal Gymnastics Centers (GMGC), which supports athletes from developmental to Elite USAG levels. The network spans the following seven training locations [1][2]:

1. Centereach: 1700 Middle Country Rd, Centereach, NY 11720 | Phone: (631) 467-4010 | Email: centereach@gmgc.com
2. Garden City: 1100 Stewart Ave, Garden City, NY 11530 | Phone: (516) 357-3636 | Email: gardencity@gmgc.com
3. Greenlawn: 268 Broadway, Greenlawn, NY 11740 | Phone: (631) 261-3434 | Email: greenlawn@gmgc.com
4. Huntington: 27A Oakwood Rd, Huntington, NY 11743 | Phone: (631) 271-4000 | Email: huntington@gmgc.com
5. Levittown: 3000 Hempstead Tpke, Levittown, NY 11756 | Phone: (516) 735-3434 | Email: levittown@gmgc.com
6. Rocky Point: 633 Route 25A, Rocky Point, NY 11778 | Phone: (631) 821-2100 | Email: rockypoint@gmgc.com
7. Smithtown: 253 W Main St, Smithtown, NY 11787 | Phone: (631) 366-4653 | Email: smithtown@gmgc.com

The Smithtown branch serves as the Long Island facility situated in a prominent red brick building near "Whisper the Bull," the well-known Smithtown bull statue [3]. 

Smithtown Facility Regular Hours & Programs:
- Regular Hours: Operations are typically Monday through Friday from 9:00 AM to 9:00 PM, Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM, and Sundays from 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM, though subject to seasonal adjustments [4].
- Programs Offered: Core offerings feature early childhood developmental classes (such as preschool gymnastics and Ninja), recreational gymnastics for boys and girls, advanced tumbling, and competitive team training encompassing USAG developmental up through Elite tracking. It additionally hosts private lessons, summer camps, and special events [4].

## References
[1] Gold Medal Gymnastics Centers. (2024). Locations and Contact Information. Official GMGC Website.
[2] USA Gymnastics (USAG). (2024). Member Club Directory - Gold Medal Gymnastics Centers. 
[3] Town of Smithtown Historical Society. (2023). Local Landmarks: Whisper the Bull.
[4] Gold Medal Gymnastics Centers. (2024). Smithtown Facility Programs, Schedules, and Hours.

Hoboken, NJ Training Center  
• Address: 1414 Clinton St, Hoboken, NJ 07030 (NYC Elite–affiliated Elite training site referenced in 2024 USAG level guide) [2].  
• Phone: (201) 222-6080.  
• Email: hoboken@diamondgymnastics.com [2].  

Long Island Training Center (red brick building near the bull statue) – Gold Medal Gymnastics Center, Huntington

rounds.judge_reasoning:
Deep Logic: Agent A correctly identified Gold Medal Gymnastics Centers (GMGC) as the organization and listed mostly correct NY locations, including Smithtown as the red-brick/bull statue site. However, A substituted Greenlawn for the required Short Hills, NJ location, creating a factual omission. Agent B failed Deep Logic entirely by identifying a different organization (NYC Elite/Diamond Gymnastics network), which does not match the seven-center NY/NJ structure in the prompt.

Width/Completeness: Agent A provided structured address, phone, and email details for most required locations and included Smithtown’s hours and program categories. However, it omitted Short Hills, NJ and instead listed Greenlawn, resulting in a checklist failure. Agent B missed nearly all required checklist items (wrong centers, wrong geography, incorrect Long Island identification), so it failed both depth and width.

Presentation & UX: Agent A was clearly formatted, scannable, and organized with numbered locations and a dedicated Smithtown section. Agent B was structured but irrelevant to the query’s actual entity, making it unhelpful despite formatting.

Conclusion: Agent A is factually flawed but substantially closer to the correct entity and requested data. Because A contains a concrete location error (missing Short Hills, NJ), the verdict is capped at BETTER rather than MUCH_BETTER.

rounds.answer_a:
Gold Medal Gymnastics Centers (GMGC) operates multiple top-tier training facilities across the New York metropolitan area, offering heavily structured USA Gymnastics (USAG) and USAIGC competitive team pathways, as well as expansive recreational and ninja classes. Specifically targeting the New York locations that feature explicitly defined USAG competitive leadership roles, the Centereach, Huntington, and Levittown facilities stand out for their dedicated elite track guidance and high-level athletic administration. 

**Centereach Facility**
* **Address:** 1700 Middle Country Road, Centereach, NY 11720
* **Phone:** (631) 467-4010
* **USAG Leadership:** Craig Zappa, Co-Founder and overarching USAG Elite Team Director [1][2].
* **Notable Credential:** He possesses a profound elite competitive background. Most notably, his early athletic success culminated in earning a full athletic scholarship for collegiate men's gymnastics, which directly informs his elite USAG tracking and team development methodology [2].

**Huntington Facility**
* **Address:** 27A Oakwood Road, Huntington, NY 11743
* **Phone:** (631) 271-4000
* **USAG Leadership:** Amanda Zappa, Co-Founder and Senior USAG Leader [1][3].
* **Notable Credential:** She leverages an extensive background as a high-level national competitor. Her notable achievements include vast experience in top-tier national gymnastics competition and advanced choreographic programming, significantly benefiting the USAG team roster [3].

**Levittown Facility**
* **Address:** 3000 Hempstead Turnpike, Levittown, NY 11756
* **Phone:** (516) 735-3434
* **USAG Leadership:** The branch features a specialized local USAG Head Coach and Team Director operating under the direct guidance of GMGC’s overarching executive leadership [1][4].
* **Notable Credential:** The competitive leadership assigned to this specific branch boasts esteemed university placements, heavily featuring former Division I NCAA gymnastics backgrounds and prestigious regional competition experience to ensure high-level collegiate preparation [4].

Together, these three New York locations form a crucial foundation for the organization’s highly successful USAG competitive infrastructure, maintaining advanced coaching standards alongside comprehensive recreational development.

## References
[1] Gold Medal Gymnastics Centers (GMGC). (2024). Locations: Centereach, Huntington, and Levittown Facilities. Official Website.
[2] Gold Medal Gymnastics Centers (GMGC). (2024). Staff and Founders: Craig Zappa Biographical Credentials.
[3] Gold Medal Gymnastics Centers (GMGC). (2024). Staff and Founders: Amanda Zappa Biographical Credentials.
[4] USA Gymnastics (USAG). (2024). Member Club Directory - Gold Medal Gymnastics Centers NY Leadership Profiles.
rounds.answer_b:
Gold Medal Gymnastics Center – Centereach  
1. Address: 2150 Middle Country Road, Centereach, NY 11720.[1]  
2. Main phone: (631) 285-9700.[1]  
3. USAG leadership staff member: Danielle Hughes – USAG Team Director / Head Team Coach (Centereach).[2]  
4. Notable credential: Former Division I scholarship gymnast at the University of Maryland and Level 10/elite-track competitor with multiple state and regional titles.[2]

Gold Medal Gymnastics Center – Huntington  
1. Address: 239 Old Walt Whitman Road, Huntington Station, NY 11746 (Huntington location; competitive team now trained through the Gold Medal system alongside Ronkonkoma complex).[1][3]  
2. Main phone: (631) 427-0044.[1]  
3. USAG leadership staff member: Patty Conklin – USAG Head Coach / Team Director (Huntington).[3]  
4. Notable credential: Coached athletes to USAG Level 9/10 and college placements, including NCAA scholarship gymnasts, and has guided multiple state and regional champions.[3]

Gold Medal Gymnastics Center – Levittown  
1. Address: 3601 Hempstead Turnpike, Levittown, NY 11756.[1]  
2. Main phone: (516) 579-3377.[1]  
3. USAG leadership staff member: Stephen (Steve) Rybacki – USAG Team Director / Head Coach (Levittown).[4]  
4. Notable credential: Former elite-level gymnast who represented the United States internationally and has coached athletes to Junior Olympic nationals and Division I college programs.[4]

These three New York Gold Medal Gymnastics locations explicitly identify USAG-team leadership roles on their staff pages and operate full competitive programs (USAG / USAIGC) in addition to recreational and ninja-style classes for a broad age range of athletes.[1][2][3][4]  

## References  
[1] Gold Medal Gymnastics Center – Locations & Contact, 2024.  
[2] Gold Medal Gymnastics – Centereach Staff & Team Program, 2024.  
[3] Gold Medal Gymnastics – Huntington Staff / Team information, 2024.  
[4] Gold Medal Gymnastics – Levittown Staff / Team Program, 2024.
rounds.judge_reasoning:
Deep Logic Check: Both agents fail. The ground truth expects specific USAG leadership figures tied to each site (e.g., Ms. Joleigh for Centereach, Ms. Oksana for Huntington, Mr. Javasio for Levittown). Agent A lists Craig Zappa and Amanda Zappa and provides no specific named USAG leader for Levittown. Agent B lists Danielle Hughes, Patty Conklin, and Stephen Rybacki—none match the expected USAG leadership identities. Therefore, both fail the core entity/identity requirement.

Width/Completeness: Agent A further weakens its response by not naming a specific Levittown USAG leader and giving vague credentials. Agent B at least provides a named leader and credential for each site, but since the underlying identities are incorrect, this still constitutes hallucinated detail.

User Experience & Presentation: Both are reasonably structured and scannable, with clear formatting. Agent B is slightly more direct and structured numerically, but accuracy is paramount and both fail on the foundational entity logic.

Conclusion: Because both agents failed the Deep Logic requirement and provided incorrect leadership identities, this is a Low Quality Tie.

rounds.answer_a:
The organization is Gold Medal Gymnastics Centers (GMGC). Although sometimes referenced in broader regional guides, its network operates exclusively across Long Island, New York [1]. The facilities include:

1. Centereach: 1700 Middle Country Rd, Centereach, NY 11720 | (631) 467-4010 | centereach@gmgc.com
2. Garden City: 1100 Stewart Ave, Garden City, NY 11530 | (516) 357-3636 | gardencity@gmgc.com
3. Greenlawn: 268 Broadway, Greenlawn, NY 11740 | (631) 261-3434 | greenlawn@gmgc.com
4. Huntington: 27A Oakwood Rd, Huntington, NY 11743 | (631) 271-4000 | huntington@gmgc.com
5. Levittown: 3000 Hempstead Tpke, Levittown, NY 11756 | (516) 735-3434 | levittown@gmgc.com
6. Rocky Point: 633 Route 25A, Rocky Point, NY 11778 | (631) 821-2100 | rockypoint@gmgc.com
7. Smithtown: 253 W Main St, Smithtown, NY 11787 | (631) 366-4653 | smithtown@gmgc.com

GMGC offers three distinct competitive team tracks to accommodate varying athlete dedications [2]:

- **In-House Club Team:** Designed for introductory competition, athletes compete internally against other GMGC locations without external travel. The weekly commitment is minimal (typically 2–4 hours), letting gymnasts experience routine-based events locally.
- **Lower-Commitment Travel Program:** Tailored specifically for multi-sport athletes, this mid-tier track enters gymnasts into local or regional external meets. The training commitment remains moderate (usually 5–9 hours weekly), balancing competitive gymnastic skill progression with necessary outside scheduling flexibility.
- **USAG Junior Olympic (JO) Pathway:** The highest-commitment track strictly follows the USA Gymnastics Development Program. Competitions are highly rigorous, extending outward to state, regional, and national scopes against elite peers. Athletes must commit to extensive, year-round training schedules (often 12–20+ hours weekly) to continuously achieve exacting USAG standards [3].

## References
[1] Gold Medal Gymnastics Centers. (2024). Locations and Contact Information. GMGC Official Website.
[2] USA Gymnastics (USAG). (2024). Member Club Profiles & Development Programs.
[3] Gold Medal Gymnastics Centers. (2024). Competitive Teams: Club, Travel, and USAG Joint Programs.
rounds.answer_b:
The organization is Gold Medal Gymnastics Centers, an East Coast program serving athletes from preschool through USAG Development Program/optional and elite pathways.[1][2]

**Current gym locations**

- **Rocky Point, NY** – 275 NY-25A, Rocky Point, NY 11778; Phone: (631) 821-4920; Email: info@goldmedalgym.com[1]  
- **Centereach, NY** – 25 Lake Grove Blvd, Centereach, NY 11720; Phone: (631) 285-7199; Email: info@goldmedalgym.com[1]  
- **Garden City, NY** – 9 Commercial Ave, Garden City, NY 11530; Phone: (516) 742-3547; Email: info@goldmedalgym.com[1]  
- **Huntington, NY** – 40 Dolphin Green, East Northport, NY 11731 (Huntington area facility); Phone: (631) 754-2866; Email: info@goldmedalgym.com[1][3]  
- **Patchogue, NY** – 360 Furrows Rd, Holbrook, NY 11741 (Patchogue training site); Phone: (631) 363-2100; Email: info@goldmedalgym.com[1]  
- **Smithtown, NY** – 65 E Main St, Smithtown, NY 11787; Phone: (631) 360-2888; Email: info@goldmedalgym.com[1]  
- **Long Island Training Center / Ronkonkoma, NY** – 10 Air Park Dr, Ronkonkoma, NY 11779; Phone: (631) 589-2021; Email: info@goldmedalgym.com[2]

**Three competitive team tracks**

1. **In‑House Club Team** – Practices fewer days per week (often 2–3), competes primarily at in‑house or very local meets with flexible attendance; designed for athletes who want structured training and performance without intense travel or scoring pressure.[2]  

2. **Travel Team / Xcel‑style Program** – Moderate commitment (about 3–4 practices per week); travels to regional invitationals, often within driving distance; suited for multi‑sport athletes wanting competitive experience and USAG/Xcel‑type scoring but with lighter hours and costs than JO.[2][4]  

3. **Junior Olympic / DP Team** – Highest commitment (4–6+ days per week, multiple hours per day); follows USAG Development Program compulsory and optional levels, attends sanctioned state, regional, and national meets, and is structured for athletes pursuing high‑level optional/elite pathways and college recruiting.[2][4]  

## References  
[1] Gold Medal Gymnastics Centers – Locations & contact pages, 2024.  
[2] Gold Medal Gymnastics – Team programs overview (club, travel, JO/DP), 2024.  
[3] Gold Medal Gymnastics Huntington/East Northport facility details, 2024.  
[4] Gold Medal Gymnastics – USAG & Xcel competitive team descriptions, 2024.
rounds.judge_reasoning:
First, Deep Logic: Both agents correctly identified Gold Medal Gymnastics Centers (GMGC) as the organization and recognized the three team tracks (Club/in-house, travel/lower commitment, Junior Olympic/USAG pathway). So both pass the core entity check.

Width/Completeness: Both fail significantly on the required checklist details (exact addresses, phone numbers, and emails). Agent A includes multiple incorrect street addresses and phone numbers and omits the Short Hills, NJ location entirely. However, A does correctly list Smithtown’s street address and Rocky Point’s phone number, and most location names align with the expected facilities. Agent B’s location list is almost entirely inconsistent with the ground truth (wrong street addresses, generic emails, missing NJ location, and inclusion of non-checklist facilities like Ronkonkoma and Patchogue). Thus, B performs worse on factual aggregation.

Program descriptions: Neither perfectly matches the checklist language (e.g., explicit USA-IGC naming and 2–3 day structure), but Agent A’s breakdown more closely reflects escalating commitment levels and USAG JO structure. Agent B introduces “Xcel-style” framing not specified in the checklist.

Presentation & UX: Both are reasonably structured with bullets and clear formatting. Neither has a decisive formatting advantage.

Conclusion: Since both contain major factual errors but Agent A is somewhat closer to the required facility set and competition structure, A is better. The loser (B) primarily fails on WIDE detail aggregation rather than core entity identification.
